hyperedges.label: Studio design showcase composing browser and iOS device frames around the portal
Guided tour definitions, runtime overlay, and Settings restart trigger
Environment Axes Govern Data Access Path
Shared Dev Worker Migrate + Deploy + Reseed Flow
Drizzle Migration Pipeline (generate to apply per target)
Shared-env proxy pattern: public API routes forward to internal routes when APP_ENV is shared
v5 schema migration cycle: initial schema, drop of deferred v1 tables, addition of v5 foundation tables
Shared-dev-sensitive files gate enforced by check-shared-dev-note.ts requiring a deploy note when schema/internal/auth files change
Portal authentication gate and member profile flow
Repository factory aggregation pattern across module facades
Placeholder repository modules sharing the empty-record-type stub pattern
Seed data definitions mapped one-to-one onto Drizzle schema tables and loaded via seed/run.ts
Shared dev token actor resolution across client and server
Authenticated upload flow: actor resolution, permission check, namespaced key assignment, storage delegation
Memory log files tracking CODE Portal planning and migration history

nodes.label: Last-super-admin self-demote guard
nodes.rationale: Prevents the sole remaining super admin from removing their own super-admin role (toggle disabled, warning shown) so the org is never left without a super admin; super-admin grant/revoke also requires confirmation and is recorded in the audit log.

nodes.label: Cochran's sample-size formula (finite population correction)
nodes.rationale: Used so admins don't have to survey every attendee — a randomly-chosen subset, sized by desired confidence and margin of error, represents the group; falls back to surveying everyone when the population is small (<=20 or sample >= population).

nodes.label: Font-gated render pattern
nodes.rationale: Mounts the React app only after Unna + Source Sans fonts finish loading (with a 2.5s safety timeout) to avoid a flash of the wider fallback serif (Georgia) which causes transient text wraps/overlap.

nodes.label: Gioia et al. (2013), Organizational identity formation and change
nodes.norm_label: gioia et al. (2013), organizational identity formation and change

nodes.label: IDEO.org (2015), The Field Guide to Human-Centered Design
nodes.norm_label: ideo.org (2015), the field guide to human-centered design

nodes.label: Cummings & Worley (2014), Organization Development and Change
nodes.norm_label: cummings & worley (2014), organization development and change

nodes.label: Lewin, K. (1947), Frontiers in Group Dynamics
nodes.norm_label: lewin, k. (1947), frontiers in group dynamics

nodes.label: Liquid glass visual recipe (blur+tint+shine)
nodes.rationale: Shared backdrop-filter blur+saturate, translucent tint, and inset shine/border combination reused across IOSGlassPill and IOSKeyboard to emulate iOS 26 'Liquid Glass' material consistently.

nodes.label: Outside Devs Get No Cloudflare Access
nodes.norm_label: outside devs get no cloudflare access

nodes.norm_label: code portal master design & build plan

nodes.norm_label: per-domain typed internal modules (not one dispatcher)

nodes.label: Shared Dev Worker Lifecycle & Redeploy Obligations
nodes.norm_label: shared dev worker lifecycle & redeploy obligations

nodes.label: articles-related table group (article_acl, article_components, article_questions, article_refs, article_related, article_sections, article_topics, dropped in v5)
nodes.norm_label: articles-related table group (article_acl, article_components, article_questions, article_refs, article_related, article_sections, article_topics, dropped in v5)

nodes.label: v5 roster sign-in gate
nodes.rationale: An email must be on the current active term's roster to sign in, unless the existing member already holds the super role, so a misconfigured or stale roster can never lock out the org's own admins. An existing member who falls off the roster is flipped to inactive so admin reporting can see they were removed, not merely never seen.

nodes.label: APP_ENV/DEPLOY_ENV validation invariants
nodes.rationale: Shared API config (SHARED_API_TOKEN, SHARED_API_BASE_URL, SHARED_API_ALLOWED_ORIGINS) must never be configured when DEPLOY_ENV=prod, and production storage must use the binding mode unless explicitly overridden, to prevent dev/shared-mode shortcuts from leaking into production.

nodes.label: shared-dev destructive-operation gate pattern
nodes.rationale: Destructive operations (member create, upload delete) are denied by contract (sharedDev: 'deny') in shared-dev mode, but authentication is still checked first so unauthenticated callers get 401 rather than silently being told the operation is disabled.

nodes.label: Reader-first public home
nodes.rationale: The root route serves public readers first while member access remains a quiet link to the portal.

nodes.label: Single portal shell
nodes.rationale: A single shell with module tabs preserves context and is easier to navigate than many disconnected member pages.

nodes.label: Unna and Source Sans 3 web fonts
nodes.norm_label: unna and source sans 3 web fonts

nodes.rationale: Middleware only redirects obvious unauthenticated portal requests because deployed D1 access was not proven and Node middleware is not supported by the Cloudflare build.

nodes.label: Authoritative server-side auth and role checks
nodes.norm_label: authoritative server-side auth and role checks

nodes.label: Clean reset path for empty D1 databases
nodes.rationale: Remote dev and production contained no user rows, so a clean reset avoids preserving an obsolete schema, subject to explicit command approval.
nodes.norm_label: clean reset path for empty d1 databases

nodes.label: Members table as Auth.js user table
nodes.norm_label: members table as auth.js user table

nodes.label: Server, database, and storage infrastructure layers
nodes.norm_label: server, database, and storage infrastructure layers

nodes.label: Cloudflare context import boundary
nodes.rationale: Only infrastructure and adapter code may access Cloudflare context so route handlers and features remain portable.

nodes.label: Shared developer Cloudflare isolation
nodes.rationale: Outside developers receive app-level shared access instead of Cloudflare account access, production credentials, or raw SQL capabilities.

nodes.label: Secret file commit and ignore policy
nodes.norm_label: secret file commit and ignore policy

nodes.label: Production storage binding lock
nodes.rationale: Production defaults to the native R2 binding and permits an override only for a documented emergency.

nodes.label: Wrangler command approval gate
nodes.rationale: Exact Wrangler commands must be shown and approved before schema resets, migrations, seeds, or production database operations.

nodes.label: Schema and contract first migration order
nodes.rationale: Schema changes land first, followed by permissions, auth, repositories, contracts, and seed data because each later layer consumes names introduced by the earlier layer.
nodes.norm_label: schema and contract first migration order

nodes.label: V5 schema additions and deferred subsystem removal
nodes.norm_label: v5 schema additions and deferred subsystem removal

nodes.label: Retention and member admin permission model
nodes.norm_label: retention and member admin permission model

nodes.label: Retention repository and deferred repository removal
nodes.norm_label: retention repository and deferred repository removal

nodes.label: Super admin roster bypass
nodes.rationale: Existing super admins bypass roster membership so a stale or misconfigured roster cannot lock out the organization administrators.

nodes.label: Local and remote migration approval gates
nodes.rationale: Migration generation may run locally, but applying migrations or redeploying the shared Worker pauses for explicit approval with exact commands.
nodes.norm_label: local and remote migration approval gates

hyperedges.label: Studio design showcase composing browser and iOS device frames around the portal
Guided tour definitions, runtime overlay, and Settings restart trigger
Environment Axes Govern Data Access Path
Shared Dev Worker Migrate + Deploy + Reseed Flow
Drizzle Migration Pipeline (generate to apply per target)
Shared-env proxy pattern: public API routes forward to internal routes when APP_ENV is shared
v5 schema migration cycle: initial schema, drop of deferred v1 tables, addition of v5 foundation tables
Shared-dev-sensitive files gate enforced by check-shared-dev-note.ts requiring a deploy note when schema/internal/auth files change
Portal authentication gate and member profile flow
Repository factory aggregation pattern across module facades
Placeholder repository modules sharing the empty-record-type stub pattern
Seed data definitions mapped one-to-one onto Drizzle schema tables and loaded via seed/run.ts
Shared dev token actor resolution across client and server
Authenticated upload flow: actor resolution, permission check, namespaced key assignment, storage delegation
Memory log files tracking CODE Portal planning and migration history
CODE Brand System
